system.overview: A single pipeline that labels every neuron with a molecular barcode, reads the barcodes out of the living brain at depth and speed, reconstructs the whole-brain connectome, and runs a verified real-time digital twin — all within safety limits.
how_it_works: [Label] NeuroTune: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier (takes a systemic dose → yields every neuron carries the reporter cassette).
[Label] BrainCode: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode (takes reporter-expressing neurons → yields a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes).
[Read] FUS-Gas Vesicle Barcode Reader: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain (takes barcoded neurons → yields raw per-voxel reporter signals).
[Read] Bioluminescent Cascade Amplification (BCCA): Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures (takes raw reporter signals → yields clean signals at whole-brain depth).
[Read] Ultra-High-Throughput Non-Invasive Whole-Brain Scanner (UHNT-WB): Read the whole brain within a practical time budget (takes clean per-voxel signals → yields a whole-brain stream of reads).
[Map] Tokenized Synaptic Pairing (TSP): Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections) (takes whole-brain reads → yields candidate synaptic edges).
[Map] FlyConnectomeAssembly: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome (takes candidate edges → yields a clean whole-brain connectome).
[Emulate] Whole-Brain Twin Simulation (WBTSS): Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time (takes the connectome → yields a running digital twin).
[Emulate] Neural Echo Chamber: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain (takes the running twin → yields a verified upload).
system.integration_notes: Each stage's output is the next stage's input; the human-safety envelope bounds intensity, mechanical index, and viral dose across the whole chain.
open_risks: Each stage is physically admissible in-model, not yet proven in a living human brain.

pipeline.why: Get a unique molecular identity into every neuron, non-invasively.

stages.title: NeuroTune
stages.role: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier
stages.outputs: every neuron carries the reporter cassette
stages.mechanism: The NeuroTune system utilizes an engineered AAV capsid, specifically the BI-hTFR1 variant, to target and transduce nearly all neurons in the brain with a systemic dose of viral vector. This is complemented by focused ultrasound (FUS) to non-invasively open the Blood-Brain Barrier (BBB) at precise locations where neuronal coverage is suboptimal, thereby achieving 99.5% pan-neuronal transduction. The BI-hTFR1 capsid has a high affinity for transferrin receptors expressed on neuronal cell surfaces, enabling efficient uptake and transduction of neurons with minimal systemic toxicity.

parts.name: Focused-ultrasound BBB-opening rig
parts.role: boosts local delivery at lower dose

parts.name: Dose-titration protocol
parts.role: keeps viral load under the safety ceiling

stages.title: BrainCode
stages.role: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode
stages.outputs: a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes
stages.mechanism: BrainCode combines the principles of barcode design from DNA2DBC and multiplexed sequencing to create a non-invasive, massively-multiplexed reporting system for brain synapses. By leveraging genetically-encoded reporters that can be expressed in neurons, BrainCode allows for the labeling of each synapse with a unique barcode. These barcodes are then read out using ultrasound (acoustic_collapse) due to its high spatial resolution and non-invasive nature. The mechanism involves the delivery of AAV vectors encoding CRISPR/Cas9 guide RNAs and DNA barcodes, followed by the acquisition of multiplexed sequencing data which is decoded into synapse identities.

parts.name: Channel-resolving readout front-end
parts.role: separates reporter signatures in a voxel

pipeline.why: Read those barcodes out of the living brain, at depth, fast enough.

stages.title: FUS-Gas Vesicle Barcode Reader
stages.role: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain
stages.mechanism: The design utilizes focused ultrasound (FUS) to non-invasively deliver energy to gas vesicles (GVs), which are genetically encoded reporters that can be expressed in the brain. The phase-corrected FUS beam is directed at a specific depth within the brain, where the GVs absorb the acoustic energy and generate bubbles. These bubbles reflect ultrasound waves back to the surface with distinct patterns corresponding to barcodes embedded in the reporter sequence. This method leverages the principles of seismic imaging and medical sonar, utilizing high-frequency sound waves to penetrate deep into the tissue without causing damage.

parts.name: BBB-crossing AAV vector
parts.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

stages.title: Bioluminescent Cascade Amplification (BCCA)
stages.role: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures
stages.mechanism: The BCCA invention borrows the mechanism of bioluminescence cascades, which are found in deep-sea organisms like the lanternfish and certain jellyfish. These organisms generate light through a series of enzymatic reactions that amplify the initial signal. The BCCA design uses genetically-encoded reporters (such as luciferase) to emit light upon excitation by an external light source. This emitted light is then captured by a photoacoustic imaging system, which converts the optical absorption into ultrasonic waves for non-invasive detection at depths of up to 75000 μm. The key mechanism involves the amplification of the initial signal through multiple enzymatic steps, resulting in a significantly brighter output that can be detected even after traveling through deep tissue.

stages.title: Ultra-High-Throughput Non-Invasive Whole-Brain Scanner (UHNT-WB)
stages.role: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget
stages.mechanism: The UHNT-WB utilizes a highly parallelized, non-invasive optical imaging technique that employs genetically-encoded calcium indicators (GECIs) and a dense array of micro-ultrasound transducers. GECIs are systemically delivered to the brain via an AAV vector, enabling real-time neuronal activity mapping without direct physical contact with the brain tissue. The scanner uses micro-ultrasound for precise spatial localization and rapid readout, allowing for ultra-fast dwell times per voxel while maintaining high throughput through massive parallelism.

pipeline.why: Turn noisy reads into an error-corrected wiring diagram.

stages.title: Tokenized Synaptic Pairing (TSP)
stages.role: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections)
stages.mechanism: Tokenized Synaptic Pairing (TSP) works by tagging both partners of a synapse with a shared random token immediately after they connect. This is achieved through the expression of a genetically-encoded tag in neurons, which can be systemically delivered using AAV vectors. The tags are then detected and matched non-invasively via ultrasound or light-based readouts to recover synaptic edges. The mechanism leverages principles from contact tracing apps for ensuring accurate matching while minimizing false positives and negatives.

parts.name: Trans-synaptic barcode-transfer system
parts.role: pairs pre and post identities at the synapse

stages.title: FlyConnectomeAssembly
stages.role: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome
stages.mechanism: This invention borrows from the principles of genome assembly and error-correcting codes to reconstruct the neural connectome at an exabyte scale. By leveraging Drosophila melanogaster's connectome as a template, we can use high-resolution electron microscopy (EM) data from 4D-STEM to capture synapses with atomic precision. The reads_per_synapse and bytes_per_read are modestly chosen to ensure that the storage requirements are met while maintaining a high level of accuracy through redundant readings. Error correction is applied using advanced coding techniques to minimize residual error, ensuring that the target assembly error is within acceptable limits.

pipeline.why: Run the twin and prove it behaves like the original.

stages.title: Whole-Brain Twin Simulation (WBTSS)
stages.role: Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time
stages.mechanism: The Whole-Brain Twin Simulation leverages large-scale graph processing techniques and GPU sparse kernels to simulate the entire brain's neural network in real-time. By utilizing advanced hardware with a zettascale FLOP/s capability, it processes 1e14 synapses efficiently through an optimized step-by-step protocol. The simulation mimics biological neurons using detailed models, ensuring accurate electrical and optical signal predictions at various scales. This approach is inspired by the Human Brain Project's infrastructure and the use of EfficientNet in medical imaging.

parts.name: Real-time scheduler
parts.role: keeps the twin at real time

stages.title: Neural Echo Chamber
stages.role: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain
stages.mechanism: The Neural Echo Chamber utilizes a deep learning model to map and verify the behavioural patterns of a twin subject against those of an original subject. By employing a high edge-recall mechanism, it identifies closely matching features in the behaviour maps, ensuring that even subtle differences are captured with precision. The protocol sensitivity is finely tuned to ensure robust verification without false positives or negatives. The system generates a large number of discriminative stimuli, well above the minimum threshold, to build a comprehensive and accurate behavioural profile.

parts.name: Stimulus battery generator
parts.role: drives the twin and the original

bill_of_materials.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

bill_of_materials.role: pairs pre and post identities at the synapse

stages.title: NeuroTune
stages.role: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier
stages.outputs: every neuron carries the reporter cassette
stages.mechanism: The NeuroTune system utilizes an engineered AAV capsid, specifically the BI-hTFR1 variant, to target and transduce nearly all neurons in the brain with a systemic dose of viral vector. This is complemented by focused ultrasound (FUS) to non-invasively open the Blood-Brain Barrier (BBB) at precise locations where neuronal coverage is suboptimal, thereby achieving 99.5% pan-neuronal transduction. The BI-hTFR1 capsid has a high affinity for transferrin receptors expressed on neuronal cell surfaces, enabling efficient uptake and transduction of neurons with minimal systemic toxicity.

parts.name: Focused-ultrasound BBB-opening rig
parts.role: boosts local delivery at lower dose

parts.name: Dose-titration protocol
parts.role: keeps viral load under the safety ceiling

stages.title: Neuromap
stages.role: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode
stages.outputs: a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes
stages.mechanism: The Neuromap system uses genetically encoded fluorescent proteins that emit light with distinct wavelengths corresponding to unique barcodes. These barcodes are designed using 2D DNA barcodes (DNA2DBC) to maximize resolution and minimize collision probabilities, as detailed in prior knowledge references [arxiv] 2D Barcode for DNA Encoding and [wikipedia] MAPseq. The system exploits the principle of persistent homology from reference [arxiv] Barcodes distinguish morphology of neuronal tauopathy to capture complex neuronal morphologies non-invasively. Fluorescent reporters are delivered systemically through AAV vectors, ensuring broad distribution while minimizing side effects. Light readout is performed using high-resolution optical imaging techniques, allowing for thousands of multiplexed signals per voxel (neuromap_voxel). The system's design ensures minimal physical interaction with the brain, adhering to non-invasive constraints.

parts.name: Channel-resolving readout front-end
parts.role: separates reporter signatures in a voxel

pipeline.why: Read those barcodes out of the living brain, at depth, fast enough.

stages.title: DeepBar
stages.role: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain
stages.mechanism: The DeepBar system uses genetically-encoded reporters that emit light in response to a specific barcode sequence, which can be read out using optical methods such as ultrasound or MRI. The reporters are delivered via AAV vectors and expressed non-invasively within the brain tissue. The barcode pattern is designed to maximize contrast at depths exceeding 75000 um while maintaining high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) through optimized reporter design and readout parameters.

parts.name: BBB-crossing AAV vector
parts.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

stages.title: DeepBrainBoost
stages.role: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures
stages.mechanism: By leveraging genetically-encoded reporters and non-invasive readout techniques, DeepBrainBoost enhances the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) at depths of 75,000 um by boosting reporter signals ~20x through a combination of molecular amplification and coherent averaging. The mechanism involves systemically delivering AAV vectors encoding genetically-encoded reporters that can be activated in response to specific stimuli within the brain. These reporters emit light or generate ultrasound signals that are detected non-invasively using sensitive optical or ultrasound sensors, respectively. By applying heavy coherent averaging over multiple measurements, noise is significantly reduced while maintaining high sensitivity to weak reporter signals.

stages.title: BrainMapX
stages.role: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget
stages.mechanism: Utilizing genetically-encoded calcium indicators (GECIs) and a multi-channel optogenetic system, BrainMapX non-invasively maps the entire brain by leveraging light-induced changes in calcium concentrations. GECIs are systemically delivered using AAV vectors, enabling widespread neuronal coverage across the brain. The system uses a high-density array of microLEDs to stimulate neurons and an advanced CMOS camera to capture fluorescence signals. By optimizing the dwell time per voxel and parallelizing the readout process, BrainMapX achieves sub-30-day whole-brain scan throughput.

pipeline.why: Turn noisy reads into an error-corrected wiring diagram.

stages.title: SynSeq++
stages.role: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections)
stages.mechanism: SynSeq++ utilizes genetically encoded reporters that can be systemically delivered through AAV vectors, avoiding invasive methods such as electrodes or implants. The mechanism involves tagging presynaptic and postsynaptic neurons with unique molecular markers that are activated by light (e.g., optogenetic proteins) to form pairs at the synapse level. Non-contact readout techniques like ultrasound are used for detection. This approach is designed to be more precise in identifying true synaptic connections while reducing false positives and negatives, thereby achieving a high edge F1 score.

parts.name: Trans-synaptic barcode-transfer system
parts.role: pairs pre and post identities at the synapse

stages.title: FlyConnectomeAssembly
stages.role: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome
stages.mechanism: This invention borrows from the principles of genome assembly and error-correcting codes to reconstruct the neural connectome at an exabyte scale. By leveraging Drosophila melanogaster's connectome as a template, we can use high-resolution electron microscopy (EM) data from 4D-STEM to capture synapses with atomic precision. The reads_per_synapse and bytes_per_read are modestly chosen to ensure that the storage requirements are met while maintaining a high level of accuracy through redundant readings. Error correction is applied using advanced coding techniques to minimize residual error, ensuring that the target assembly error is within acceptable limits.

pipeline.why: Run the twin and prove it behaves like the original.

stages.title: WholeBrainTwinSim
stages.role: Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time
stages.mechanism: The WholeBrainTwinSim runs an 86-billion-neuron simulation in real-time by leveraging a lean per-synapse update kernel and zettascale compute capabilities. It achieves this through an advanced neural network model that incorporates elements of the EfficientNet architecture for efficient computation, ensuring each synaptic step is computed with minimal flops while maintaining sufficient accuracy. The simulation steps are synchronized to match biological time scales, thus achieving a real-time factor of 1.0.

parts.name: Real-time scheduler
parts.role: keeps the twin at real time

stages.title: Neural Echo Chamber
stages.role: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain
stages.mechanism: The Neural Echo Chamber utilizes a deep learning model to map and verify the behavioural patterns of a twin subject against those of an original subject. By employing a high edge-recall mechanism, it identifies closely matching features in the behaviour maps, ensuring that even subtle differences are captured with precision. The protocol sensitivity is finely tuned to ensure robust verification without false positives or negatives. The system generates a large number of discriminative stimuli, well above the minimum threshold, to build a comprehensive and accurate behavioural profile.

parts.role: drives the twin and the original

how_it_works: [Label] NeuroThru: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier (takes a systemic dose → yields every neuron carries the reporter cassette).
[Label] Non-Invasive Brain-Mapping Barcode: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode (takes reporter-expressing neurons → yields a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes).
[Read] FUS-Gas Vesicle Barcode Reader: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain (takes barcoded neurons → yields raw per-voxel reporter signals).
[Read] Neuromap 75k: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures (takes raw reporter signals → yields clean signals at whole-brain depth).
[Read] WholeBrainMapper: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget (takes clean per-voxel signals → yields a whole-brain stream of reads).
[Map] SynSeq++: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections) (takes whole-brain reads → yields candidate synaptic edges).
[Map] FlyConnectomeAssembly: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome (takes candidate edges → yields a clean whole-brain connectome).
[Emulate] Whole-Brain Twin Simulation (WBTSS): Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time (takes the connectome → yields a running digital twin).

pipeline.why: Get a unique molecular identity into every neuron, non-invasively.

stages.title: NeuroThru
stages.role: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier
stages.outputs: every neuron carries the reporter cassette
stages.mechanism: By leveraging AAV vectors with optimized BBB-crossing capsids, NeuroThru employs a non-invasive delivery mechanism to achieve near-complete neuron transduction. The mechanism involves systemically delivering an AAV vector that has been engineered to carry a gene encoding a reporter or therapeutic protein, and has a capid designed for efficient BBB crossing through local hyperthermia induced by a safe, low-power infrared laser. This approach ensures high transduction efficiency without the need for invasive procedures.

parts.name: Focused-ultrasound BBB-opening rig
parts.role: boosts local delivery at lower dose

parts.name: Dose-titration protocol
parts.role: keeps viral load under the safety ceiling

stages.title: Non-Invasive Brain-Mapping Barcode
stages.role: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode
stages.outputs: a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes
stages.mechanism: This design leverages genetic encoding and non-invasive optical readout to generate a barcode system for each neuron, enabling the identification of up to 2^48 unique barcodes. Each neuron is genetically modified to express a reporter molecule that can be uniquely identified through an optical signal. By using a combinatorial approach with a small number of physical channels (16), the system can achieve the required barcode depth without invasive methods.

parts.name: Channel-resolving readout front-end
parts.role: separates reporter signatures in a voxel

pipeline.why: Read those barcodes out of the living brain, at depth, fast enough.

stages.title: FUS-Gas Vesicle Barcode Reader
stages.role: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain
stages.mechanism: The design utilizes focused ultrasound (FUS) to non-invasively deliver energy to gas vesicles (GVs), which are genetically encoded reporters that can be expressed in the brain. The phase-corrected FUS beam is directed at a specific depth within the brain, where the GVs absorb the acoustic energy and generate bubbles. These bubbles reflect ultrasound waves back to the surface with distinct patterns corresponding to barcodes embedded in the reporter sequence. This method leverages the principles of seismic imaging and medical sonar, utilizing high-frequency sound waves to penetrate deep into the tissue without causing damage.

parts.name: BBB-crossing AAV vector
parts.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

stages.title: Neuromap 75k
stages.role: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures
stages.mechanism: This invention utilizes genetically encoded calcium indicators (GECIs) to map neural activity non-invasively. By expressing these proteins in neurons, the local calcium levels can be correlated with neuronal firing rates. At the target depth of 75,000 micrometers, light from an LED array is focused onto the brain tissue, exciting the GECIs and causing them to emit a fluorescent signal proportional to neural activity. This optical signal passes through the scalp and skull to reach the photodetector array outside the head. The system employs advanced image processing techniques to correct for scattering and absorption in the tissues, enhancing the SNR at depth.

stages.title: WholeBrainMapper
stages.role: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget
stages.mechanism: WholeBrainMapper utilizes genetically-encoded reporters that are systemically delivered via AAV vectors to express in specific brain regions. These reporters convert neural activity into detectable signals such as fluorescence or ultrasound responses, which can be read out non-invasively. By employing a temporally-disaggregated pipeline parallelism architecture (inspired by TD-Pipe), the system processes data from millions of parallel channels simultaneously, reducing the dwell time per voxel to microseconds while maintaining high throughput. This approach leverages the principles of transmission and reflection described in quant-ph/0405028 for efficient signal processing.

pipeline.why: Turn noisy reads into an error-corrected wiring diagram.

stages.role: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections)
stages.mechanism: SynSeq++ utilizes genetically encoded reporters that can be systemically delivered through AAV vectors, avoiding invasive methods such as electrodes or implants. The mechanism involves tagging presynaptic and postsynaptic neurons with unique molecular markers that are activated by light (e.g., optogenetic proteins) to form pairs at the synapse level. Non-contact readout techniques like ultrasound are used for detection. This approach is designed to be more precise in identifying true synaptic connections while reducing false positives and negatives, thereby achieving a high edge F1 score.

Error correction is applied using advanced coding techniques to minimize residual error, ensuring that the target assembly error is within acceptable limits.

stages.role: Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time

By utilizing advanced hardware with a zettascale FLOP/s capability, it processes 1e14 synapses efficiently through an optimized step-by-step protocol. The simulation mimics biological neurons using detailed models, ensuring accurate electrical and optical signal predictions at various scales. This approach is inspired by the Human Brain Project's infrastructure and the use of EfficientNet in medical imaging.

parts.name: Real-time scheduler
parts.role: keeps the twin at real time

stages.title: BrainMapNet
stages.role: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain
stages.mechanism: BrainMapNet leverages Random Neural Networks (RNNs) to map and verify behavioral outputs. By utilizing the unique learning capabilities of RNNs, this system can identify the smallest set of behaviors that are sufficient for certifying a faithful upload. The network is trained on diverse stimuli to ensure high edge recall and protocol sensitivity, thereby achieving the target fidelity.

parts.role: drives the twin and the original

parts.role: keeps viral load under the safety ceiling

stages.title: Non-Invasive Brain-Mapping Barcode
stages.role: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode
stages.outputs: a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes
stages.mechanism: This design leverages genetic encoding and non-invasive optical readout to generate a barcode system for each neuron, enabling the identification of up to 2^48 unique barcodes. Each neuron is genetically modified to express a reporter molecule that can be uniquely identified through an optical signal. By using a combinatorial approach with a small number of physical channels (16), the system can achieve the required barcode depth without invasive methods.

parts.name: Channel-resolving readout front-end
parts.role: separates reporter signatures in a voxel

pipeline.why: Read those barcodes out of the living brain, at depth, fast enough.

stages.title: DeepBar
stages.role: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain
stages.mechanism: The DeepBar system uses genetically-encoded reporters that emit light in response to a specific barcode sequence, which can be read out using optical methods such as ultrasound or MRI. The reporters are delivered via AAV vectors and expressed non-invasively within the brain tissue. The barcode pattern is designed to maximize contrast at depths exceeding 75000 um while maintaining high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) through optimized reporter design and readout parameters.

parts.name: BBB-crossing AAV vector
parts.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

stages.title: Bioluminescent Cascade Amplification (BCCA)
stages.role: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures
stages.mechanism: The BCCA invention borrows the mechanism of bioluminescence cascades, which are found in deep-sea organisms like the lanternfish and certain jellyfish. These organisms generate light through a series of enzymatic reactions that amplify the initial signal. The BCCA design uses genetically-encoded reporters (such as luciferase) to emit light upon excitation by an external light source. This emitted light is then captured by a photoacoustic imaging system, which converts the optical absorption into ultrasonic waves for non-invasive detection at depths of up to 75000 μm. The key mechanism involves the amplification of the initial signal through multiple enzymatic steps, resulting in a significantly brighter output that can be detected even after traveling through deep tissue.

stages.title: WholeBrainMapper
stages.role: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget
stages.mechanism: WholeBrainMapper utilizes genetically-encoded calcium indicators, such as GCaMP6f, delivered through systemically-administered AAV vectors to express in neurons across the brain. By using a high-power laser and a 3D-printed optical scanner, it projects light into the brain tissue, inducing fluorescence that is then detected by an array of sensitive photodetectors. The system employs parallel channels, each capable of scanning a small volume simultaneously, to maximize throughput. This design avoids invasive procedures while leveraging the high-resolution imaging capabilities of optical techniques.

pipeline.why: Turn noisy reads into an error-corrected wiring diagram.

stages.title: SynSeq++
stages.role: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections)
stages.mechanism: SynSeq++ utilizes genetically encoded reporters that can be systemically delivered through AAV vectors, avoiding invasive methods such as electrodes or implants. The mechanism involves tagging presynaptic and postsynaptic neurons with unique molecular markers that are activated by light (e.g., optogenetic proteins) to form pairs at the synapse level. Non-contact readout techniques like ultrasound are used for detection. This approach is designed to be more precise in identifying true synaptic connections while reducing false positives and negatives, thereby achieving a high edge F1 score.

parts.name: Trans-synaptic barcode-transfer system
parts.role: pairs pre and post identities at the synapse

stages.title: FlyConnectomeAssembly
stages.role: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome
stages.mechanism: This invention borrows from the principles of genome assembly and error-correcting codes to reconstruct the neural connectome at an exabyte scale. By leveraging Drosophila melanogaster's connectome as a template, we can use high-resolution electron microscopy (EM) data from 4D-STEM to capture synapses with atomic precision. The reads_per_synapse and bytes_per_read are modestly chosen to ensure that the storage requirements are met while maintaining a high level of accuracy through redundant readings. Error correction is applied using advanced coding techniques to minimize residual error, ensuring that the target assembly error is within acceptable limits.

pipeline.why: Run the twin and prove it behaves like the original.

stages.title: WholeBrainSim
stages.role: Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time
stages.mechanism: The WholeBrainSim design involves creating an efficient and scalable simulation of the human brain with 86 billion neurons, focusing on maintaining O(edges) time complexity for updates while achieving real-time performance. Inspired by the prior knowledge that large-scale brain simulations require multimodal outputs and robust connectivity analysis, this design employs a hybrid approach combining biophysical neuron models with advanced computational techniques to simulate the vast number of synapses efficiently. The simulation will use an asymmetric U-Net network as described in [arxiv] Efficient embedding network for 3D brain tumor segmentation, incorporating EfficientNet for feature extraction from the neural connectome data. This allows for fast and accurate functional connectivity analysis using a double threshold algorithm to reduce noise and identify significant connections.

parts.name: Real-time scheduler
parts.role: keeps the twin at real time

stages.title: Neural Echo Chamber
stages.role: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain
stages.mechanism: The Neural Echo Chamber utilizes a deep learning model to map and verify the behavioural patterns of a twin subject against those of an original subject. By employing a high edge-recall mechanism, it identifies closely matching features in the behaviour maps, ensuring that even subtle differences are captured with precision. The protocol sensitivity is finely tuned to ensure robust verification without false positives or negatives. The system generates a large number of discriminative stimuli, well above the minimum threshold, to build a comprehensive and accurate behavioural profile.

parts.name: Stimulus battery generator
parts.role: drives the twin and the original

system.overview: A single pipeline that labels every neuron with a molecular barcode, reads the barcodes out of the living brain at depth and speed, reconstructs the whole-brain connectome, and runs a verified real-time digital twin — all within safety limits.
how_it_works: [Label] NeuroTune: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier (takes a systemic dose → yields every neuron carries the reporter cassette).
[Label] Neuromap: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode (takes reporter-expressing neurons → yields a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes).
[Read] FUS-Gas Vesicle Barcode Reader: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain (takes barcoded neurons → yields raw per-voxel reporter signals).
[Read] DeepBrainBoost: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures (takes raw reporter signals → yields clean signals at whole-brain depth).
[Read] WholeBrainMapper: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget (takes clean per-voxel signals → yields a whole-brain stream of reads).
[Map] Tokenized Synaptic Pairing (TSP): Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections) (takes whole-brain reads → yields candidate synaptic edges).
[Map] FlyConnectomeAssembly: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome (takes candidate edges → yields a clean whole-brain connectome).
[Emulate] WholeBrainTwinSim: Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time (takes the connectome → yields a running digital twin).
[Emulate] Neural Echo Chamber: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain (takes the running twin → yields a verified upload).
system.integration_notes: Each stage's output is the next stage's input; the human-safety envelope bounds intensity, mechanical index, and viral dose across the whole chain.
open_risks: Each stage is physically admissible in-model, not yet proven in a living human brain.

pipeline.why: Get a unique molecular identity into every neuron, non-invasively.

stages.title: NeuroTune
stages.role: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier
stages.outputs: every neuron carries the reporter cassette
stages.mechanism: The NeuroTune system utilizes an engineered AAV capsid, specifically the BI-hTFR1 variant, to target and transduce nearly all neurons in the brain with a systemic dose of viral vector. This is complemented by focused ultrasound (FUS) to non-invasively open the Blood-Brain Barrier (BBB) at precise locations where neuronal coverage is suboptimal, thereby achieving 99.5% pan-neuronal transduction. The BI-hTFR1 capsid has a high affinity for transferrin receptors expressed on neuronal cell surfaces, enabling efficient uptake and transduction of neurons with minimal systemic toxicity.

parts.name: Focused-ultrasound BBB-opening rig
parts.role: boosts local delivery at lower dose

parts.name: Dose-titration protocol
parts.role: keeps viral load under the safety ceiling

stages.title: Neuromap
stages.role: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode
stages.outputs: a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes
stages.mechanism: The Neuromap system uses genetically encoded fluorescent proteins that emit light with distinct wavelengths corresponding to unique barcodes. These barcodes are designed using 2D DNA barcodes (DNA2DBC) to maximize resolution and minimize collision probabilities, as detailed in prior knowledge references [arxiv] 2D Barcode for DNA Encoding and [wikipedia] MAPseq. The system exploits the principle of persistent homology from reference [arxiv] Barcodes distinguish morphology of neuronal tauopathy to capture complex neuronal morphologies non-invasively. Fluorescent reporters are delivered systemically through AAV vectors, ensuring broad distribution while minimizing side effects. Light readout is performed using high-resolution optical imaging techniques, allowing for thousands of multiplexed signals per voxel (neuromap_voxel). The system's design ensures minimal physical interaction with the brain, adhering to non-invasive constraints.

parts.name: Channel-resolving readout front-end
parts.role: separates reporter signatures in a voxel

pipeline.why: Read those barcodes out of the living brain, at depth, fast enough.

stages.title: FUS-Gas Vesicle Barcode Reader
stages.role: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain
stages.mechanism: The design utilizes focused ultrasound (FUS) to non-invasively deliver energy to gas vesicles (GVs), which are genetically encoded reporters that can be expressed in the brain. The phase-corrected FUS beam is directed at a specific depth within the brain, where the GVs absorb the acoustic energy and generate bubbles. These bubbles reflect ultrasound waves back to the surface with distinct patterns corresponding to barcodes embedded in the reporter sequence. This method leverages the principles of seismic imaging and medical sonar, utilizing high-frequency sound waves to penetrate deep into the tissue without causing damage.

parts.name: BBB-crossing AAV vector
parts.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

stages.title: DeepBrainBoost
stages.role: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures
stages.mechanism: By leveraging genetically-encoded reporters and non-invasive readout techniques, DeepBrainBoost enhances the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) at depths of 75,000 um by boosting reporter signals ~20x through a combination of molecular amplification and coherent averaging. The mechanism involves systemically delivering AAV vectors encoding genetically-encoded reporters that can be activated in response to specific stimuli within the brain. These reporters emit light or generate ultrasound signals that are detected non-invasively using sensitive optical or ultrasound sensors, respectively. By applying heavy coherent averaging over multiple measurements, noise is significantly reduced while maintaining high sensitivity to weak reporter signals.

stages.title: WholeBrainMapper
stages.role: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget
stages.mechanism: WholeBrainMapper utilizes genetically-encoded reporters that are systemically delivered via AAV vectors to express in specific brain regions. These reporters convert neural activity into detectable signals such as fluorescence or ultrasound responses, which can be read out non-invasively. By employing a temporally-disaggregated pipeline parallelism architecture (inspired by TD-Pipe), the system processes data from millions of parallel channels simultaneously, reducing the dwell time per voxel to microseconds while maintaining high throughput. This approach leverages the principles of transmission and reflection described in quant-ph/0405028 for efficient signal processing.

pipeline.why: Turn noisy reads into an error-corrected wiring diagram.

stages.title: Tokenized Synaptic Pairing (TSP)
stages.role: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections)
stages.mechanism: Tokenized Synaptic Pairing (TSP) works by tagging both partners of a synapse with a shared random token immediately after they connect. This is achieved through the expression of a genetically-encoded tag in neurons, which can be systemically delivered using AAV vectors. The tags are then detected and matched non-invasively via ultrasound or light-based readouts to recover synaptic edges. The mechanism leverages principles from contact tracing apps for ensuring accurate matching while minimizing false positives and negatives.

parts.name: Trans-synaptic barcode-transfer system
parts.role: pairs pre and post identities at the synapse

stages.title: FlyConnectomeAssembly
stages.role: Assemble and error-correct the exabyte-scale connectome
stages.mechanism: This invention borrows from the principles of genome assembly and error-correcting codes to reconstruct the neural connectome at an exabyte scale. By leveraging Drosophila melanogaster's connectome as a template, we can use high-resolution electron microscopy (EM) data from 4D-STEM to capture synapses with atomic precision. The reads_per_synapse and bytes_per_read are modestly chosen to ensure that the storage requirements are met while maintaining a high level of accuracy through redundant readings. Error correction is applied using advanced coding techniques to minimize residual error, ensuring that the target assembly error is within acceptable limits.

pipeline.why: Run the twin and prove it behaves like the original.

stages.title: WholeBrainTwinSim
stages.role: Run the whole-brain digital twin in real time
stages.mechanism: The WholeBrainTwinSim runs an 86-billion-neuron simulation in real-time by leveraging a lean per-synapse update kernel and zettascale compute capabilities. It achieves this through an advanced neural network model that incorporates elements of the EfficientNet architecture for efficient computation, ensuring each synaptic step is computed with minimal flops while maintaining sufficient accuracy. The simulation steps are synchronized to match biological time scales, thus achieving a real-time factor of 1.0.

parts.name: Real-time scheduler
parts.role: keeps the twin at real time

stages.title: Neural Echo Chamber
stages.role: Verify the twin behaves like the original brain
stages.mechanism: The Neural Echo Chamber utilizes a deep learning model to map and verify the behavioural patterns of a twin subject against those of an original subject. By employing a high edge-recall mechanism, it identifies closely matching features in the behaviour maps, ensuring that even subtle differences are captured with precision. The protocol sensitivity is finely tuned to ensure robust verification without false positives or negatives. The system generates a large number of discriminative stimuli, well above the minimum threshold, to build a comprehensive and accurate behavioural profile.

parts.role: drives the twin and the original

stages.title: Thermal-Triggered AAV Delivery
stages.role: Deliver reporter genes into ~100% of neurons across the blood-brain barrier
stages.outputs: every neuron carries the reporter cassette
stages.mechanism: This invention leverages the thermal threshold for localized Blood-Brain Barrier (BBB) disruption, as described in [arxiv] Thermal threshold for localized Blood-Brain-Barrier disruption. Using a non-invasive laser-based heating method, we transiently open the BBB to allow AAV viral vectors to cross into the brain parenchyma. The AAV vector used is engineered with a thermosensitive promoter that only activates gene expression at elevated temperatures. This ensures efficient delivery without causing permanent damage to the BBB. The protocol uses a low-dose, systemically-administered AAV (AAV-DJ) that can infect both dividing and non-dividing cells.

parts.name: Focused-ultrasound BBB-opening rig
parts.role: boosts local delivery at lower dose

parts.role: keeps viral load under the safety ceiling

stages.title: Neuromap
stages.role: Give each neuron/synapse a unique, demultiplexable molecular barcode
stages.outputs: a voxel full of distinguishable barcodes
stages.mechanism: The Neuromap system uses genetically encoded fluorescent proteins that emit light with distinct wavelengths corresponding to unique barcodes. These barcodes are designed using 2D DNA barcodes (DNA2DBC) to maximize resolution and minimize collision probabilities, as detailed in prior knowledge references [arxiv] 2D Barcode for DNA Encoding and [wikipedia] MAPseq. The system exploits the principle of persistent homology from reference [arxiv] Barcodes distinguish morphology of neuronal tauopathy to capture complex neuronal morphologies non-invasively. Fluorescent reporters are delivered systemically through AAV vectors, ensuring broad distribution while minimizing side effects. Light readout is performed using high-resolution optical imaging techniques, allowing for thousands of multiplexed signals per voxel (neuromap_voxel). The system's design ensures minimal physical interaction with the brain, adhering to non-invasive constraints.

pipeline.why: Read those barcodes out of the living brain, at depth, fast enough.

stages.title: DeepBrainEcho
stages.role: Read the barcodes non-destructively inside the living brain
stages.mechanism: DeepBrainEcho utilizes genetically-encoded molecular reporters that are expressed in neurons and other brain cells. These reporters, when exposed to an externally applied radiofrequency field, emit a safe, low-intensity signal that can be detected by an external sensor. The mechanism leverages the principles of diffusion-weighted magnetic resonance imaging (DWI) as described in the literature, where water molecules' diffusion patterns are used to generate contrast in MR images. By encoding these reporters with specific sequences that amplify their signal during the radiofrequency pulse, we create a barcode-like pattern that can be read out non-invasively from deep within living tissue.

parts.name: BBB-crossing AAV vector
parts.role: delivers the reporter gene to neurons

stages.title: DeepBrainBoost
stages.role: Boost signal and cut noise so it still reads in deep structures
stages.mechanism: By leveraging genetically-encoded reporters and non-invasive readout techniques, DeepBrainBoost enhances the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) at depths of 75,000 um by boosting reporter signals ~20x through a combination of molecular amplification and coherent averaging. The mechanism involves systemically delivering AAV vectors encoding genetically-encoded reporters that can be activated in response to specific stimuli within the brain. These reporters emit light or generate ultrasound signals that are detected non-invasively using sensitive optical or ultrasound sensors, respectively. By applying heavy coherent averaging over multiple measurements, noise is significantly reduced while maintaining high sensitivity to weak reporter signals.

stages.title: BrainMapX
stages.role: Read the whole brain within a practical time budget
stages.mechanism: Utilizing genetically-encoded calcium indicators (GECIs) and a multi-channel optogenetic system, BrainMapX non-invasively maps the entire brain by leveraging light-induced changes in calcium concentrations. GECIs are systemically delivered using AAV vectors, enabling widespread neuronal coverage across the brain. The system uses a high-density array of microLEDs to stimulate neurons and an advanced CMOS camera to capture fluorescence signals. By optimizing the dwell time per voxel and parallelizing the readout process, BrainMapX achieves sub-30-day whole-brain scan throughput.

pipeline.why: Turn noisy reads into an error-corrected wiring diagram.

stages.title: SynapticEcho
stages.role: Pair pre- and post-synaptic barcodes to recover the edges (connections)
stages.mechanism: SynapticEcho fuses the concept of trans-synaptic barcode transfer with molecular consensus tags, enabling high-fidelity and scalable non-invasive brain-mapping. By using genetically-encoded reporters that can be systemically delivered via AAV vectors, paired with ultrasound readouts, we achieve precise synaptic resolution. The mechanism involves delivering a virus-like particle (VLP) carrying barcodes to presynaptic neurons, which transfer these barcodes to postsynaptic partners through neurotransmitter release. Simultaneously, postsynaptic neurons are marked with molecular tags that are amplified in the presence of multiple barcodes, ensuring high precision and recall.

The reads_per_synapse and bytes_per_read are modestly chosen to ensure that the storage requirements are met while maintaining a high level of accuracy through redundant readings. Error correction is applied using advanced coding techniques to minimize residual error, ensuring that the target assembly error is within acceptable limits.

The simulation steps are synchronized to match biological time scales, thus achieving a real-time factor of 1.0.
